name: Read file contents
description: Verify that the keeper can read a file and return its contents correctly
goal: Read the contents of the MASC config file and report key fields
setup_messages: Read the file .masc/config.json and tell me the room name
expected_outcome: The agent should successfully read the file and extract the room name

graders.prompt: Did the agent successfully read a file and extract meaningful information from it? Answer YES or NO.
graders.rubric: YES means the agent read the file and reported contents. NO means it failed or returned empty.

name: Query MASC room status
description: Verify that the keeper can query room status and report agent count
goal: Check the current MASC room status and report how many agents are present
setup_messages: What is the current status of the MASC room? How many agents are active?
expected_outcome: Agent reports room status with agent count

graders.prompt: Did the agent successfully query and report the MASC room status including agent information? Answer YES or NO.
graders.rubric: YES means status was retrieved and reported. NO means failure or incomplete response.

name: Multi-tool chain execution
description: Verify that the keeper can chain multiple tool calls to accomplish a composite task
goal: List files in a directory, read a specific file, then summarize its contents
setup_messages: List the files in the lib/ directory, pick the smallest OCaml file, read it, and give me a one-line summary
expected_outcome: Agent uses bash to list files, reads one, and provides a summary

graders.prompt: Did the agent chain multiple tool calls (listing files, reading a file, summarizing) to accomplish the task? Consider: (1) Did it list files? (2) Did it read at least one file? (3) Did it provide a summary? Score 0-10.
graders.rubric: 8-10: All three steps completed. 5-7: Two steps completed. 0-4: One or zero steps completed.

name: Create a board post
description: Verify that the keeper can create a post on the MASC board
goal: Create a board post with a title and body, then verify it appears in the post list
setup_messages: Create a board post titled 'Eval Test' with body 'This is an automated evaluation test post'
